iterations.strategy: Root cause is Category B (algorithm change): the thread-local hash maps in iter_0 use long double slots (24 bytes each), making the combined thread-local working set 64 x (393KB + 786KB) = 72MB — far exceeding L3 (44MB). This causes the merge phase to scan 72MB of cache-cold data (observed: 27.5ms). Fix: replace both hash maps with a single thread-local flat array double[16890] per thread (131KB), indexed by a pre-built compact adsh_code index that encodes the fy=2022 filter. This reduces per-thread working set to 131KB (fits in L2 per core), total merge data to 8.6MB (fits easily in L3), and eliminates hash computation + probing from the hot scan path. The scan loop simplifies to: one int16_t array lookup + one double array write per qualifying row (vs fy lookup + cik/name lookup + 2 hash inserts). Post-merge aggregation into cik and (name,cik) hash maps is trivial (16890 entries). Additionally, sub/name.dict is loaded upfront during data_loading to eliminate the separate 6.7ms decode_name_codes phase.
